# Strategic Roadmap — 2026-06-11

**Author:** Claude (Fable 5), strategic analysis session 2026-06-11
**Basis:** Full read of brain/decisions/ (2026-05-25 → 2026-06-11), signals.json, findings.json (51 findings), strategy-registry.md, integration-contract.md v2.8, all strategy-notes/ pre-registrations, MASTER_HANDOVER_2026-06-10, kpis.md, and git logs of both repos (first-repo + trading-swarm). Every dated claim below is verifiable against those files.

---

## 1. Trajectory Verification — Does the Scorecard Reflect the Current System?

**Verdict: No. The scored signals were all generated by architectures that have since been replaced or substantially augmented. But the honest corollary is equally important: the *current* architecture has zero scored signals either way. The scorecard is not evidence of failure; it is also not evidence of success. The system is pre-validation, not failing.**

### 1.1 The infrastructure timeline (verified via git log on both repos)

| Date | Capability added | Commit evidence |
|------|------------------|-----------------|
| 2026-05-25 | **geo_elo v2** (market-implied probability ELO) + geo_directionality_score; STR-003 qualification switched from comprehensive_elo to geo_elo | session summary 05-25; `74ffef0` (05-29) |
| 2026-05-30 | Anti-arb entry price filter (0.10–0.80); `score_str003_signals.py` (automated outcome scoring); transaction-hash dedup | `dd7b1bf`, `c9608fd` |
| 2026-06-02 | **geo_elo_active** (180-day recency decay) — dormant high-ELO traders no longer qualify | `002f70e` |
| 2026-06-03 | geo_elo_active bug fixes (timestamp source, datetime parsing, thin-trade skip); corrected P&L filter; 229 ARB_BOT wallets excluded | `aa99c71`, `2ba2d45`, `54bb28d` |
| 2026-06-04–05 | Resolution pipeline overhaul: 180 stale markets resolved via CLOB, CLOB second pass in `fast_resolution_check.py`, automated ARB_BOT detection | `4c88cef`, `fb380f4`, `631a2f6` |
| 2026-06-06 | Template audit (wrong JOIN keys silently dropping 37% of trades — fixed); schema-change-log created; pre_resolution_loop timing bug fixed (it had **never fired** between ~May 10 and June 6) | `f380109`, `fb2c88b`, `c62b903` |

### 1.2 Signal-by-signal cross-reference

**STR003-003 — Warsh Fed NO — WRONG.** Fired 2026-04-27 under **comprehensive_elo** criteria (trader comprehensive_elo 3,323; **geo_elo NULL** — the metric did not exist until May 25). More damning: the market had **already resolved on April 4**, 23 days before the signal fired — the resolution pipeline of April could not see it. Three separate pieces of current infrastructure would have prevented this signal entirely: geo_elo qualification (May 25, trader would not qualify), the resolution sweep overhaul (June 4–8), and `resolve_legendary_markets.py` (June 9). This failure is a *data-pipeline* failure of an architecture that no longer exists, not a forecasting failure.

**STR003-001 (Newsom NO) and STR003-004 (Putin NO)** — both fired pre-geo_elo (April 21 / May 25). Both traders **fail the current geo_elo threshold** (geo_elo 1,461 and 1,554 vs 2,175 required). They are retained for outcome tracking only — the system itself has marked them `ACTIVE_BELOW_THRESHOLD` / thin-sample. Whatever they resolve to on Sept 1 / June 30, they say nothing about the current qualification bar.

**STR003-005 (Keiko YES — CORRECT) and STR003-006 (López Aliaga YES — WRONG).** Fired together 2026-06-04 — the **first genuine geo_elo_active signals in system history**, five days before SCS existed, six days before archetype profiles. Both were single-trader signals from 0xecaa8806 on the *same election* — a mutual-exclusivity conflict the system documented on June 6 but has not yet automated. Three later-built capabilities are directly responsive: SCS would have scored both LOW (single trader, no consensus — explicitly noted in the June 10 scoring decision record); the external-dataset cross-reference (June 6) revealed 0xecaa8806 is **76% maker / 74% both-sides** — a profile-level red flag invisible at signal time; archetype × domain weighting (June 10) now gates exactly this kind of inclusion.

**STR003-009 (Graham SC NO — WRONG).** Fired 2026-06-09 — the only failure with *partial* current infrastructure. SCS existed and scored it 43.2 MEDIUM (annotation-only by pre-registration). Honesty requires noting: SCS did **not** flag this as LOW, so SCS does not fully exonerate it. What does stand out: total LEGENDARY capital behind the signal was **$129.94**. The system had (and still has) no minimum-capital threshold on scan-derived signals. Archetype weighting (built the next day) and a capital floor are the responsive fixes.

**STR-004 founding case (Russia/Ukraine ceasefire — WRONG).** Registered 2026-05-08 using **comprehensive_elo > 2175** legendary definition — a tier the system has since formally measured at **46–49% accuracy on contested markets** (worse than random; findings 2026-05-25-ELO-LEGENDARY-001, 2026-06-05-CONTESTED-ACCURACY). STR-004 as currently specified aggregates capital from a contaminated pool with no archetype filter — 17 of the 37 profiled LEGENDARY/near-LEGENDARY traders are YIELD_HARVESTERs whose capital carries no predictive information. The 0/1 record tests a specification that should not accumulate more cases without re-registration (see §4.4).

### 1.3 The honest conclusion

- Of the 4 scored STR-003 signals, **zero** were generated under the current stack (geo_elo_active + anti-arb + SCS + archetype weighting + net-position scan + conflict awareness). Two predate geo_elo entirely; two predate SCS/archetypes by 5–6 days.
- The single CORRECT signal (Keiko) is *also* not validation of the current stack — it was a single-trader signal the current stack would have downgraded. The 1/4 record is uninformative in both directions.
- Gate 3's "29+ days stalled" is mechanical, not evaluative: the pre_resolution_loop **never fired between ~May 10 and June 6** (timing bug, fixed June 6 — confirmed by the file gap in `brain/agent-outputs/pre-resolution/`: scans exist 05-05→05-10, then nothing until 06-06), and **no outcome-scoring mechanism for STR-002 signals has ever been built**. The gate is stalled because the measurement loop doesn't exist, not because the strategy measured 50% and stopped.
- The capability curve is steep and real: between May 25 and June 11 the system replaced its qualification metric, fixed its resolution pipeline, expanded Pool C 7× (402 → 2,848) via category sync, built consensus-credibility scoring, profiled its entire elite pool, and gave itself temporal memory. Signals fired from June 12 onward are the first that test the system as it now exists.
- **The first genuine test of the current system is June 30** (STR003-007 Iran NO, 7 LEGENDARY, HIGH confidence; STR003-008 Europe NO, 2 LEGENDARY, MEDIUM), plus whatever the June 15 trader-intelligence run and subsequent Monday scans register.

## 2. The Complete Strategic Picture

### 2.1 The system in one paragraph

The system's thesis is: *skilled geopolitics traders exist, are identifiable via category-specific market-implied-probability ELO, and their conviction positions carry information the market price has not absorbed.* Everything in flight is one of four things: (a) infrastructure to **identify** those traders cleanly (pools, ELO, archetypes, bot/LP exclusion), (b) mechanisms to **detect** their conviction (STR-001→005, positions scan, SCS), (c) machinery to **verify honestly** whether detected signals carry edge (pre-registration, scoring scripts, gates, feedback loop), and (d) the **self-improvement loop** that turns failures into filters (the June infrastructure burst is this loop working).

### 2.2 Strategy inventory (verified against strategy-registry.md + signals.json)

| Strategy | Status | Record | What it actually tests | Disposition |
|----------|--------|--------|------------------------|-------------|
| STR-001 (elite convergence) | SUSPENDED | 56.1% (n=41) | Whether 3+ legendary traders converging predicts outcome | Dead — 78% of markets triggered both sides; root cause (LPs hold both sides) now structurally understood |
| STR-001b (exclusive convergence) | SUSPENDED | 0 qualifying signals | Convergence with opposition filter | Dormant — premise absorbed into SCS two-sidedness penalty |
| STR-002 (pre-resolution divergence) | EXPERIMENTAL | 50% / 4 markets (stale) | ELO-weighted smart-money consensus vs price, ≤7 days to resolution | **Gate 3 carrier — blocked on a scoring loop that doesn't exist** |
| STR-003 (single legendary directional) | EXPERIMENTAL | 1/4 scored; 2 active (June 30); 2 legacy outcome-only | Whether one qualified trader's ≥95% conviction beats the market | **Primary strategy**; current-stack validation begins now |
| STR-004 (capital-weighted aggregate) | HYPOTHESIS | 0/1 | Whether aggregate legendary capital divergence ≥20pp beats the crowd | Needs re-specification (geo_elo + archetype weights) before more cases |
| STR-005 (extreme tail arbitrage) | HYPOTHESIS (note only) | — | Structural mispricing of scary tails; no ELO dependency | Unstarted; cheap to historically validate; diversifying because it's not trader-skill-dependent |
| LH-001 / insider track | CONDITIONAL_PASS | 4/7 (57.1%) — fails 60% blocking item | Whether new single-event high-volume wallets near resolution are informed money | Watchlist trigger only; blocked on independent-event accumulation (needs 5+ events; has 2) |

### 2.3 The research pipeline (pre-registrations in brain/strategy-notes/)

**Gate-critical:**
- **RQ1.1** (ELO in T predicts Brier in T+1) — twice INCONCLUSIVE on sample size (n=16 April, n=10 June 1 self-halt, need 30). Rerun **July 1**. Now has elo_snapshots for clean point-in-time ELO going forward. *Stopping rule: if it fails with adequate n, the ELO premise itself is redesigned.*
- **RQ3.2** (geo_elo LEGENDARY consensus ≥3 traders > market price) — pre-registered 05-29, needs 20+ resolved consensus signals; estimated July–Sept. The June 10 pool expansion (LEGENDARY active 11 → 18–22) materially accelerates accumulation — STR003-007 (7 LEGENDARY agreeing) is exactly the signal shape RQ3.2 needs.

**July 1 cycle (currently 6–8 RQs stacked on one date — see §4.5 for sequencing):** RQ-CONTESTED-001 (difficulty-weighted K), RQ-POOL-QUALITY-001 (market-diversity filter for LEGENDARY), RQ-SECTOR-001 (archetype × domain — now has empirical data from profiling), RQ-EXEC-001 (execution vs forecasting; preliminary result favors forecasting), RQ-LH-001, RQ-PNLGATE-001, RQ-ILS-001.
**August:** RQ-EXT-001a/b/c (external dataset validation — re-run 08-01 after seed cohort matures), RQ-CONVICTION-001 (frac_held_to_resolution).
**Now-runnable:** RQ-SCI-001 (SCS vs raw trader count on *historical resolved* markets — does not need to wait).
**Phase 4+:** RQ4-MULTI (multivariate Kelly), RQ4.1/4.2 (Kelly alignment — in research-directions.md, not yet pre-registered).

### 2.4 Phase 5 gates — what each actually means

| Gate | Status | What it really verifies |
|------|--------|------------------------|
| 1. feedback-loop 4+ weekly runs | ✅ MET (10 runs by June 5) | The self-improvement loop physically operates |
| 2. 3+ HIGH findings, n≥20 | ✅ MET (June 5; ~17–18 qualifying of 22 raw HIGH) | The research machinery can produce statistically grounded knowledge |
| 3. STR-002 ≥60% on 10+ markets | ❌ STALLED | A *daily, prospective, automatically scored* signal stream beats a bar — **blocked on missing scoring loop, not on measured failure** |
| 4. RQ1.1 AND RQ3.2 pass | ❌ PENDING | The two stopping-rule questions: ELO skill persists; elite consensus beats price. These are the *premise* of the whole system |

### 2.5 The dependency graph

```
trades ingestion ─► category sync (06-10) ─► resolution pipeline ─► trade_result eval
                                                                        │
                  external dataset (06-06) ─► trader discovery          ▼
                  (195 external_seed + 16 watchlist)            geo_elo / geo_elo_active
                                                                        │
                                              Pool C (2,848) ─► LEGENDARY set (18–22 active clean)
                                                                        │
                                          archetype profiles (37) ──────┤
                                                                        ▼
                              legendary_positions_scan + signal-agent ─► STR-003 signals
                                                                        │
                                                   SCS annotation ──────┤
                                                                        ▼
                                          signals.json registration ─► score_str003_signals
                                                                        │
       elo_snapshots (06-11) ─► counter-signal detection (≥06-18)       ▼
                            ─► RQ1.1 (07-01)                    findings.json / registry
                            ─► drift detection (trader-intel,           │
                               first run 06-15)                          ▼
                                                              feedback-loop ─► Phase gates
```

Read the graph bottom-up for what blocks what: **Gate 3 blocks on an STR-002 scoring loop (unbuilt). Gate 4 blocks on calendar + data accumulation (July 1 / 20 consensus signals). Counter-signal detection blocks on snapshot history (7 days minimum → earliest June 18). RQ-SECTOR/POOL-QUALITY block on July 1 by pre-registration discipline. Everything else upstream is now built and running.**

### 2.6 What the 51 findings / ~18 HIGH collectively establish

Three load-bearing results, all replicated at n≥100:
1. **ELO tiers carry real information on contested markets** — +20–31pp over market baseline on 0.35–0.65 priced markets (n=746, 2026-06-03-ELO-VS-MARKET-001); QUALIFIED 66.3% on 2026 contested (n=101).
2. **Category-specific ELO ≫ comprehensive ELO** — geo_elo LEGENDARY 67–78% where comprehensive_elo LEGENDARY is 46–49% (worse than random) on the same market classes. The June 3 formula-bias analysis explains *why* (ROI-based actual_score rewards near-certainty volume 2.3×).
3. **Pool C consensus is accurate in aggregate** — 70.7% on all 444 resolved 2026 geo/elections markets; 86.4% on the 22-market 30-day window.

And one critical *negative* result the system deserves credit for producing about itself: **raw ELO rank is a poor signal-quality proxy** (17/37 profiled elites are yield harvesters; the directionality filter correlates −0.135 with actual both-sidedness). That finding is the bridge from "ELO leaderboard" to "trader intelligence," and it is the most important conceptual advance since geo_elo itself.

---

## 3. Honest Assessment

### 3.1 On track toward the north star?

**Directionally yes, with one structural caveat.** The north star is a self-improving multi-agent system with genuine edge in geopolitics markets. The *self-improving* half is demonstrably working — the May 25 → June 11 record is a textbook loop of failure → diagnosis → structural fix (Warsh → resolution pipeline; Peru → conflict detection + SCS + archetypes; Graham → capital floors needed; Iran consensus overcount → net-position scan). The *genuine edge* half is **unproven and currently rests on in-sample evidence**. That is not a criticism — it is exactly where a system mid-Phase 5 should be — but no one should mistake pipeline maturity for validated edge. The two stopping-rule questions (RQ1.1, RQ3.2) remain open, and the system's own rules say everything halts if they fail.

### 3.2 What is actually working

- **geo_elo tier discrimination** — replicated, large-n, and the contested-market slice rules out the easiest contamination story.
- **The archetype insight** — operationally the biggest June win; converts "who has high ELO" into "whose positions mean something."
- **The temporal layer** — elo_snapshots closes the worst epistemic hole the system had (it could not reconstruct what it knew at signal time). Post-mortems, RQ1.1, drift and counter-signal detection all become possible.
- **Pre-registration discipline** — it has held under pressure repeatedly (RQ1.1 self-halting twice rather than p-hacking; SCS shipped annotation-only; LH-001 v1 p=0.0000 corrected to a confound in v2). This is rarer and more valuable than any single finding.
- **The immune system / contract machinery** — SCL log catching wrong JOIN keys that silently dropped 37% of trades is the kind of failure that kills quant systems invisibly; this one caught it.

### 3.3 What is genuinely stuck vs. what merely looks stuck

**Genuinely stuck (needs building or decision, won't resolve with time):**
1. **Gate 3 / STR-002 scoring loop** — does not exist. Nothing will change until `score_str002_signals.py` (or equivalent) is built. This is the single highest-leverage unbuilt item in the system.
2. **STR-002 signal quality itself** — the June 11 scan flags markets at gaps of 80–97pp (e.g., "smart money 98.8% YES vs market 18%") with zero ELITE/LEGENDARY participation. Divergences that extreme are far more likely stale-position artifacts than information. When a scoring loop is built, expect the naive QUALIFIED-tier divergence signal to score *badly* — that would be the gate doing its job, but be prepared for it (§5.1).
3. **STR-004 specification** — accumulating cases under comprehensive_elo legendary + no archetype filter is accumulating noise.
4. **Signal schema bifurcation** — 001–006 vs 007–009 formats; partially canonicalised (06-11 backfill commit `348aa6d`), needs finishing before any automated scoring depends on it.

**Looks stuck, is actually timing (no action needed beyond patience + calendar):**
- Gate 4 (July 1 rerun; RQ3.2 accumulation now accelerated by the 7× pool expansion).
- STR-003 accuracy (n=4, none under current stack; June 30 batch + post-June-12 signals are the real series).
- LH-001 (needs independent events; 2 exist).
- external_seed cohort (avg 7.1 resolved geo trades; RQ-EXT-001 correctly deferred to Aug 1).
- Counter-signal detection (needs ≥7 days of snapshots; earliest June 18 — by design).

### 3.4 Where the system risks fooling itself

1. **Accuracy-vs-60% is the wrong yardstick, and the system already proved it.** The STR-001 post-mortem recorded "market price 100% accurate; signal adds no edge." A NO signal registered when the market is at 0.97 NO will be "correct" 97% of the time and worth nothing. Several current/incoming signals have this shape (STR003-007 was registered after the market had already moved 32% → 1.25%; the Iran scan note even says "signal largely validated — market moved"). **Price movement after entry is not validation; resolution-relative-to-entry-price is.** The signal schema now records `market_price_at_registration` — but the gates and scoring scripts still consume raw accuracy. Until scoring is market-relative (e.g., Brier vs market-price baseline, or excess log-score at registration price), the headline accuracy number can look ≥60% with zero economic edge — or look <60% while edge exists (contrarian signals). This is the most dangerous metric trap in the system.
2. **Finding-count inflation.** The ~18 HIGH findings include weekly re-runs of the same tier-accuracy analysis (QUALIFIED: 81% → 92% → 65% → 71% → 55% across five weeks). Those oscillations mean noise and/or non-stationarity, and re-counting weekly snapshots as accumulating independent evidence overstates the knowledge base. Gate 2's three *qualifying* findings are solid; the "18" headline is soft.
3. **The flagship trader was not what the model assumed.** STR-003's premise is conviction takers; its founding signal generator (0xecaa8806) is 76% maker. The archetype layer answers this, but it is a standing warning: every assumption about *why* a trader is accurate should be checked against behavior before their capital is treated as information.
4. **In-sample geo_elo accuracy includes yield-harvest trades.** The 67–78% LEGENDARY figures aggregate all geo trades, including near-certainty entries. The honest question Gate-4-adjacent research must answer: **what is geo_elo LEGENDARY accuracy on contested markets only, excluding YIELD_HARVESTERs?** That number does not exist yet. It might be the single most informative number the system could produce in July.
5. **Discovery-layer survivorship.** external_seed traders were selected on past P&L (`pnl_taker_politics > $10K`). Selected-on-performance cohorts regress; their forward accuracy will be lower than their backward stats. RQ-EXT-001's prospective design handles this *if* the comparison is strictly out-of-sample from selection.
6. **2025-H2 is a poisoned well and April 28 2026 is a structural break.** The system already found 2025-H2 accuracy of 25% on contested markets, and the platform changed regime on April 28 (V2 exchange, pUSD, CFTC US launch, sharp-money influx — see pending scout items dated 06-11). All calibrations should be reported pre/post April 28; anything trained across the boundary is suspect.
7. **The Keiko scoring precedent.** STR003-005 was scored CORRECT at 0.97 market price before official resolution. Pragmatic, but it created an unwritten rule. Write it down: provisional scoring allowed only at price >0.95 with high volume, flagged `provisional: true`, confirmed at oracle resolution. Otherwise scoring discipline will erode case by case.

---

## 4. The Roadmap

### 4.0 Phase numbering note

CLAUDE.md's table lists "Phase 4 Paper Trading" before "Phase 5 Integration," but the system has in practice run integration-gate work first and paper trading has not begun. This document adopts the operative numbering: **Phase 5 = validation gates (current), Phase 6 = paper trading / shadow book, Phase 7 = live pilot, Phase 8 = scaled self-improving operation.** Recommend Oscar update CLAUDE.md's phase table to match.

### 4.1 Finish Phase 5 (target: September 2026)

**Gate 3 plan (the critical path, ~2 weeks of building + ~3 weeks of accumulation):**
1. Build `score_str002_signals.py` mirroring the STR-003 scoring protocol: each pre-resolution scan signal gets a signal_id, market_id, direction, tier, market_price_at_registration, and outcome fields. Dedupe across daily scans (same market+direction = one signal, first-seen date).
2. Score both raw accuracy (the gate metric as written) **and** market-relative edge (Brier vs price baseline) from day one — the gate is honored as registered, but the economically meaningful number rides alongside.
3. Backfill from the June 6 scan restart onward (markets resolving June 8–15 are already scoreable). With ~11 signals/day on ≤7-day markets, 10+ resolved markets accumulate within 2–3 weeks of the scoring loop existing.
4. Accept the result. If the naive divergence signal scores <60%, Gate 3 has done its job: STR-002 then needs a redesign (tier filter to LEGENDARY/ELITE only, staleness filter on the smart-money positions, minimum participation) and a fresh accumulation window — do not lower the gate.

**Gate 4 plan:**
- **RQ1.1, July 1:** verify n≥30 *before* the run (the pool expansion and category sync make this likely but not certain; June 1 had n=10 — if still short, re-register for Aug 1 rather than running underpowered). Run the RQ0.2 bot screen first (due June 13 per kpis.md).
- **RQ3.2:** begin formal prospective collection **now** — every legendary_positions_scan consensus signal (≥3 LEGENDARY, same side, archetype-filtered) is an RQ3.2 observation. STR003-007 already qualifies. At current pool size, 20 resolved consensus signals by late August is realistic. Score market-relative as well.
- If either stopping rule fires with adequate power, **honor it** — halt strategy work, redesign the ELO premise or pivot the edge source. That commitment is what distinguishes this system from the 170 competitors.

**June 30 checkpoint (calendar-forced):** STR003-004, 007, 008 all resolve. Note they are *correlated* — all NO on Iran/Russia escalation-adjacent events (the system's own RQ-CORRELATION-001 flagged the shared-worldview risk on June 9). Score them individually but treat them as ~1.5 independent observations, not 3.

### 4.2 Validating the June infrastructure on live signals (the "v2 cohort")

None of SCS, archetype weighting, profiles, snapshots, or the net-position scan has touched a *scored* signal yet. Define a clean validation cohort:

- **Cohort definition:** every signal registered on or after 2026-06-12 carrying the canonical schema (SCS score, archetype-filtered trader list, market_price_at_registration, elos_at_registration from snapshots).
- **Track separately** from the legacy cohort (001–009). Never blend the accuracy series; the legacy series is an architectural fossil record.
- **Success looks like (by end of Phase 6):** ≥60% raw accuracy AND positive market-relative edge on ≥20 resolved v2 signals; SCS-HIGH signals outperform SCS-LOW (RQ-SCI-001 prospective leg); archetype-filtered signal set outperforms unfiltered counterfactual (compute both on every signal — the counterfactual is free).
- **RQ-SCI-001 historical leg can run immediately** on resolved markets — it needs no waiting and would let SCS graduate from annotation-only with evidence rather than time.

### 4.3 Phase 6 — Paper Trading / Shadow Book (entry: Phase 5 gates met; duration: 8–12 weeks)

The gap between "signals score well" and "money compounds" is execution, sizing, and portfolio behavior. Phase 6 builds and tests all three with zero capital at risk:

- **Entry criteria:** all 4 Phase 5 gates met; v2 cohort ≥10 resolved with positive market-relative edge; signal schema fully canonical.
- **Build:** a *swarm-side* portfolio ledger (our positions, orders, fills — the system tracks thousands of traders' positions but has no concept of its own); order-book depth capture at signal time (see §5.2 — start capturing **now**, paper trading needs the history); a fill simulator that fills only at observed book liquidity (limit orders only, partial fills, no fantasy mid-price fills); fractional Kelly sizing (¼ Kelly cap) with per-market, per-category, and correlation-cluster caps; drawdown tracking.
- **Exit criteria (all required):** ≥30 paper trades resolved; positive P&L after simulated spread/slippage; max drawdown <20% of paper bankroll; calibration curve sane (predicted edge vs realized); no risk-limit breach unexplained; Kelly sizing logic audited against RQ4.1 groundwork.
- **Stop rule:** paper P&L negative after 30 resolved trades → return to Phase 5 posture, diagnose whether the loss is edge decay, execution cost, or sizing.

### 4.4 Phase 7 — Live Pilot (entry: Phase 6 exit; duration: 12+ weeks)

- **Entry criteria:** Phase 6 exit met; execution layer built against **V2 CLOB / pUSD** (the April 28 migration means every example and wrapper written against V1 is dead — verify before writing code); anonymous funded wallet with documented ops procedure; kill switch tested; runbook section added for live-trading failures.
- **Operating rules:** minimal capital (e.g., $1–2K); limit orders only (hard rule); max 5 concurrent positions; ≥50% bankroll in reserve; one correlation cluster ≤2 positions; auto-halt on 15% drawdown or any scoring-pipeline outage >48h (if the system can't score itself, it doesn't trade).
- **Exit criteria:** ≥30 live resolved trades; live edge within confidence bounds of paper edge (execution costs explained); zero hard-rule violations; Oscar's review of every loss ≥5% of bankroll.
- **Strategy portfolio at this stage:** STR-003 v2 as primary book; STR-002 (if Gate 3 passed and redesign held) as secondary; STR-004 only if re-registered under geo_elo + archetype weights and 10 fresh cases scored ≥60%; STR-005 historically validated by then or dropped; LH-001 stays a watchlist trigger feeding attention, never sizing.

### 4.5 Phase 8 — Scaled Self-Improving Operation

- Capital scaling tied to realized-edge confidence intervals (scale only when the lower bound of the edge estimate clears costs), never to recent P&L.
- Multiple independent books (trader-skill edge, structural-mispricing edge, event-driven insider watchlist) with portfolio-level Kelly (RQ4-MULTI graduates here).
- Automated strategy lifecycle: feedback-loop agent flags decay → backtest-agent revalidates → Oscar retires. (The machinery exists; Phase 8 is when it governs real capital.)
- Continuous trader-pool management as a first-class production system: discovery (Layer 3 /holders sweep), drift detection, archetype re-profiling, pool half-life monitoring — the trader-intelligence-agent matures into the system's core asset (see §5.6).

### 4.6 Sequencing the July research cycle (currently overloaded)

Eight RQs are stacked on July 1. Sequence by dependency, not by filing date:

- **Wave 1 (July 1–7) — "who counts":** RQ-POOL-QUALITY-001 and RQ-SECTOR-001 first (they define the qualified pool and weights every other test should use), then **RQ1.1** (gate-critical, snapshot-based) and **RQ-CONTESTED-001**.
- **Wave 2 (mid-July) — "which filters help":** RQ-EXEC-001, RQ-PNLGATE-001, plus the new must-run: *geo_elo LEGENDARY accuracy on contested markets, archetype-filtered* (pre-register it now; it is the number that connects the tier findings to STR-003's premise).
- **Wave 3 (August) — "external validation":** RQ-EXT-001a/b/c (Aug 1), RQ-CONVICTION-001, RQ-ILS-001 (it needs order-flow infrastructure anyway — pair with order-book capture work).
- **Continuous:** RQ3.2 accumulation, RQ-SCI-001 prospective leg, RQ-CORRELATION-001 measurement on the June 30 cluster outcome.

---

## 5. What's Been Overlooked — Hurdles, Missing Components, and the Endgame

### 5.1 Hurdles not yet on the board

1. **The accuracy-without-edge trap is structural, not incidental (restating §3.4.1 as a build item).** Make market-relative scoring a schema-level requirement: every scoring script computes `edge_at_entry = outcome - market_price_at_registration` (signed by direction) and a Brier-vs-market delta. Gates and dashboards should display both. A 70%-accurate signal stream with zero average edge-at-entry is a tracking system, not a trading system.
2. **Detection latency eats edge silently.** STR003-007 was found *retrospectively* after the market had already moved 32% → 1.25%. Weekly Monday scans + daily maintenance is a batch cadence; informed-money edges on geopolitical events decay in hours–days. Measure it: record `price_at_trader_entry` vs `price_at_signal_registration` on every signal — the gap is edge the cadence burned. If it's large (it will be), the production answer is event-driven detection: a trade-stream trigger that fires when any qualified LEGENDARY/GENUINE_FORECASTER takes a new net position ≥ threshold, rather than waiting for the Monday scan. This is the single biggest gap between "research pipeline" and "production system."
3. **Hindsight contamination in retrospective signal registration.** A signal registered *after* its favorable move was visible cannot be counted in validation statistics without lookahead bias. Rule to adopt now: signals discovered retrospectively are tagged `non_scorable_for_validation` (track them, learn from them, but exclude from gate/accuracy series). Only signals registered before material price movement count toward validation.
4. **Reflexivity / crowding.** Legendary wallets are publicly observable on-chain; the leaderboard-copying ecosystem is large, and competitive-moat.md itself counts 170+ competitors. If followers move price immediately after legendary entries, the copyable edge at *our* entry price is smaller than the trader's edge at theirs. Measurable with existing data: price impact in the hours after qualified-trader entries. Should be measured before Phase 6 sizing assumptions are set.
5. **Capacity is small and should be named.** Contested geo markets are thin (the Graham signal's entire LEGENDARY capital was $130; Iran consensus was $27K). Even a fully validated edge may support only $10–50K of deployed capital before the system becomes the market it is measuring. That's compatible with the mission — but it bounds the endgame and argues for the multi-book diversification in §4.5/§5.7.
6. **Statistical power of the gates.** 6/10 correct clears a 60% gate but happens 38% of the time by coin-flip; even 12/20 happens ~25%. The gates as written verify machinery more than edge. Keep them (they were pre-registered), but the *internal* standard for Phase 7 entry should be a binomial lower bound: e.g., ≥65% observed on n≥30 with market-relative edge >0, which is roughly where p<0.05 vs coin-flip lands. Cheap to compute, hard to fool.
7. **Single-machine, single-DB existential risk.** One 6.3GB SQLite file on one mini-PC, WAL-lock cascades already observed, backup retention recently *reduced* to 3 days, no offsite copy mentioned anywhere in the brain. The trade history and elo_snapshots are irreplaceable (the external parquet covers only V1/older data, and Gamma backfill has known caps). **An encrypted nightly offsite backup (DB + brain/) is the cheapest insurance in the entire roadmap and should predate everything else in this section.**
8. **Oracle and resolution risk as a permanent tax.** Peru took weeks (ONPE); UMA disputes exist; markets resolve "unknown"; the system has 52 overdue LEGENDARY markets clearing slowly. Phase 6+ must model resolution lag (capital locked in resolved-but-unsettled positions) and the provisional-scoring rule from §3.4.7 needs to be written into the contract.
9. **Platform regime risk is live right now.** Pending scout items (06-11): Polymarket US API public, V2/pUSD migration with **no backward compatibility**, plus fee/collateral changes. Beyond the integration to-do, the deeper issue: every historical calibration spans a platform that no longer exists in that form. Treat 2026-04-28 as a hard structural-break date in all analyses (echoes the 2025-H2 25% finding — the system has already seen one regime break destroy a calibration).
10. **Same-event signal conflation.** The mutual-exclusivity gap (Peru) is documented but unbuilt, and June 30's three correlated NO signals show the portfolio version of the same problem. One "event cluster" field on signals (shared election/conflict identifier) solves both the conflict flag and the correlation cap with one schema change.

### 5.2 Missing components a production system needs (none exist yet)

In rough build order:

1. **Offsite backup** (§5.1.7). Days of work, existential payoff.
2. **Market-relative scoring** in all scoring scripts + gates dashboards (§5.1.1).
3. **STR-002 scoring loop** (§4.1 — Gate 3's literal blocker).
4. **Order-book depth capture at signal registration.** Start now even though trading is quarters away: Phase 6's fill simulator is only as honest as its book history, and book history cannot be backfilled. A small script snapshotting the CLOB book (top 10 levels both sides) for every market that enters signals.json, at registration and daily until resolution.
5. **Event-driven signal detection** (§5.1.2) — the batch→stream upgrade.
6. **Counter-signal detector** (designed, blocked until ~June 18 by snapshot accumulation — keep it scheduled; Peru showed counter-signals must *downweight*, never auto-invalidate).
7. **Swarm-side portfolio ledger + risk engine** (positions/orders/fills/limits for *us*) — Phase 6's skeleton.
8. **Execution layer** against V2 CLOB/pUSD with the official Python SDK — Phase 7's skeleton; evaluate the new SDK (scout item) rather than hand-rolling.
9. **Capital allocation / Kelly module** — fractional Kelly with correlation clusters; RQ4.1/4.2 groundwork exists in research-directions.md but nothing is pre-registered; do that during Phase 6, not after.
10. **A backtesting harness that actually replays time.** The 7-sins checklist + validate_backtest.py enforce thresholds, but with elo_snapshots the system can now do what it never could: replay any past date with *only* the knowledge available that day (point-in-time ELO, pool membership, archetype). A snapshot-driven replay harness turns every future strategy proposal from "in-sample analysis" into "as-if-live simulation." This is the highest-leverage research infrastructure the temporal layer unlocks.

### 5.3 A phase the roadmap is missing: the Calibration Layer

Signals are currently directional (YES/NO + confidence tier). Real sizing needs probabilities. Between Phase 5 and live capital, build the layer that converts (signal features: SCS, archetype mix, capital, entry price, latency, event class) → calibrated probability of being correct, fitted on the accumulating scored-signal history and validated on reliability curves. Without it, Kelly sizing in Phase 6 has no probability input and will silently default to flat sizing or gut feel. This can grow organically (start with simple bucketed empirical rates by SCS tier × archetype), but it must exist before sizing does.

### 5.4 The trader-intelligence asset is bigger than the signal layer

The system's most defensible moat is not any single strategy — it is the longitudinal, archetype-aware, point-in-time-snapshotted model of *who is smart money in geopolitics markets*. Strategies (STR-003 etc.) are read-heads on that asset. Two implications: (a) invest in the asset's quality (drift detection, re-profiling cadence, discovery layers) even when a given strategy stalls; (b) the asset compounds — every resolved market improves it, while individual strategies can be arbed away. If one thing should survive a failed RQ3.2, it is this layer (a stopping-rule failure would mean elite consensus doesn't beat *price* — the trader model could still have value for event detection, watchlists, or research products).

### 5.5 Creative/endgame stepping stones (post-validation)

- **An in-house forecaster as a second edge source.** The swarm has model routing to frontier LLMs. Run an independent LLM forecast (with news retrieval) on every signal market, Brier-track it against both the market and the trader signals. Three uncorrelated opinions (market price, smart money, model) make a better ensemble than any alone — and the model's track record accumulates for free alongside signals. Cheap experiment; pre-register it like everything else.
- **Cross-platform expansion (Kalshi, deferred correctly in May)** re-enters at Phase 7+ as *hedging and divergence* infrastructure rather than a new signal source: the same event priced on two venues bounds true probability and occasionally gifts riskless spreads.
- **STR-005 as the uncorrelated mechanical book** — no ELO dependency, complements skill-based books; one historical scan validates or kills it cheaply.
- **Intelligence as a product.** The weekly positions-scan + archetype digest is already close to a publishable "smart money in geopolitics" intelligence brief. If trading capacity caps out (§5.1.5), the asset has a second monetization path that doesn't consume the edge.
- **RL position sizing (research-directions Direction 7)** stays Phase 8+ — only after the calibration layer has a year of data. Anything earlier is curve-fitting with extra steps.

### 5.6 The one-sentence summary

The scorecard measures a system that no longer exists; the system that exists has not yet been measured — June 30 and the v2 cohort begin that measurement, Gate 3 needs its scoring loop built this week, market-relative scoring must replace raw accuracy before any gate is trusted, and the road to real capital runs through a shadow book whose order-book history should start recording today.
